title: Jefferson, Monticello
description: This quiz is for the essay Jefferson, Monticello.
topic_path: Art of the Americas to World War I
British Colonies to the Early Republic

assessment_items.question_content:
**Jefferson is remembered as one of the earliest supporters of  [[☃ dropdown 1]] architecture in the United States.**

hints:
**Jefferson is remembered as one of the earliest supporters of  neoclassical architecture in the United States.**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Jefferson was inspired by which two architects in the building of Monticello?**


![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/198166a35aa6fe8860db9f5455ab2074.png)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Jefferson was inspired by the classical architecture of Antonio Palladio and James Gibbs.**

**[Read this essay to review](/test-prep/ap-art-history/later-europe-and-americas/enlightenment-revolution/a/jefferson-monticello)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Jefferson believed that classical architecture reinforced the ideals behind the classical past. Which of the following is NOT a classical ideal?**


![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/198166a35aa6fe8860db9f5455ab2074.png)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Democracy, education, and civic responsibility are all classical ideals.**

**[Read this essay to review](/test-prep/ap-art-history/later-europe-and-americas/enlightenment-revolution/a/jefferson-monticello)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Jefferson altered Monticello as a result of viewing [[☃ dropdown 1]]  [[☃ dropdown 2]] architecture.**


![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/198166a35aa6fe8860db9f5455ab2074.png)
hints:
****Jefferson altered Monticello as a result of viewing French neoclassical architecture.**
